items.headline: City Council reviews 2026-27 capital spending priorities
items.summary: Mountain View is planning its next fiscal year's infrastructure and project investments. The study session allows residents to see what physical improvements and developments the city is prioritizing for the coming year.

items.headline: Sunnyvale considers new tenant protection rules
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission is introducing an ordinance to amend the city's Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and responsibilities in Sunnyvale apartments and rental homes.

items.headline: San José releases new Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan
items.summary: The city presented a status report on an updated plan for library facilities and customer experience improvements. This could signal changes to library services, hours, or physical upgrades across San José's branch locations.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets bonus square footage for renovation
items.summary: The building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is being elevated to higher historic protection status, which qualifies it for an additional 5,000 square feet of development space as an incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A proposal to rezone and redevelop 788-790 and 796 San Antonio Road would bring an 8-story building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 square feet of commercial space. The project requires City Council approval and represents significant new housing near a major commercial corridor.

items.headline: City Council Authorizes Construction Contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was authorized to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully specified in the agenda. Residents may want to follow up on what this construction project entails and where it will be located in Los Gatos.

items.headline: City awards contract for 2026 street repaving project
items.summary: The council approved bids and awarded a contract for the 2026 Local Streets Remove and Replace Pavement Project. Residents should watch for street maintenance work in their neighborhoods over the coming months.

items.headline: City Council updates Active Transportation Plan with new scoring criteria
items.summary: The council reviewed Phase 2 of Cupertino's Active Transportation Plan, including revised scoring criteria and plan edits. This plan will shape future biking and pedestrian infrastructure investments across the city.
